channel.title: Justinesnacks (mela-rss)
channel.description: Auto-curated recipes from Justinesnacks (mela-rss), ready to import into Mela.

item.title: Gochujang Tofu Mince
item.description: This recipe is a simple way to get a ton of plant-based protein, and it's extremely flavorful, too. A common worry with crumbled tofu is that it will stick and burn to your pan, but this recipe uses a quick deglazing trick to make sure all those crispy, delicious brown bits end right where they need to be - which is hopefully in your dinner. — 3 servings · PT25M

item.title: Sungold Tomato Orzo with Crispy Basil Garlic
Texas Sheet Cake Ice Cream Sandwiches
item.description: While Texas Sheet Cake is a classic, I always like to riff on a classic. I recommend a bitter ice cream flavor like coffee to be the center of these, they can be intensely rich (just like Texas Sheet Cake itself!). If you have chocoholics in your life - this one is sure to hit the spot. — 12 servings · PT13H40M

item.title: Basil Tomato Salmon
item.description: A bright, light, and extra easy summer meal. You only need one burner, which makes it ideal for hot summer days. This works with any cherry tomato variety you have on hand, or even vine tomatoes. The salmon skin gets irresistibly crispy, so make sure to keep it skin up after you initially sear it. The result is worth the (minimal) effort. — 3 servings · PT40M

item.title: Pan con Tomate
item.description: While I love any quick summer snack, this one has to be my all-time favorite. It's so simple, and while I stray from the traditional Spanish technique, the result is just as easy and delicious. — 2 servings · PT15M

item.title: Sizzling Harissa Eggplant with Chickpeas
item.description: This easy weeknight recipe is a great fit for the grill or the oven. Eggplant does most of the work, roasting down into a tender, pull-apart base where all the spicy-sweet harissa sauce can soak in. I like to scoop out the sizzly interior with a spoon and serve it with rice, but you can eat this plant-based dinner any way your heart desires. — 4 servings · PT1H

item.title: Ginger Turmeric Hummus with Sungold Tomatoes
item.description: For when you want hummus with a little something extra, this ginger turmeric hummus comes right in. The spicy, bright hummus pairs perfectly with a topping of sweet and jammy sungold tomatoes, making it a good dip for crackers, pita, crudité or even chips. There's just enough contrast that you'll be coming back for bite after bite. — 8 servings · PT15M

item.title: Gooey Butter Cake Ice Cream Sandwiches
item.description: This is a fun spin on a St. Louis classic. This recipe makes two ultra-thin "gooey butter cakes" and uses them as the outside layers for ice cream sandwiches. What you get is a super-plush ice cream sandwich which tastes like a frozen gooey butter cake. And in the summertime, that sounds pretty perfect to me. — 12 servings · PT5H35M

item.title: Miso Corn Rice with Chili Cod
item.description: There's a natural sweetness to corn that pairs really well with miso and scallions, which I love cooking down into a tender, fluffy rice. Putting a chili-glazed cod on top only takes a bit more time (and not much effort), and it leaves you with a balanced summer dinner that all takes place in one pan. — 4 servings · PT45M

item.title: Smashed Cucumber Spritz
item.description: When there are so many spritzes to choose from, try this herbaceous, refreshing cucumber one. It takes barely any work to make a natural cucumber syrup, and the flavor is both clean and refreshing. Perfect for a brunch, a cook out, a dinner party or for sipping by the pool. — 4 servings · PT1H10M

item.title: Cucumber and Mango Salad
item.description: Crisp, cool and crunchy cucumber is the perfect partner for juicing mango, especially when there's something sweet and something spicy in the mix. This salad is lightly dressed, but it stays cool and refreshing - perfect for all your poolside salad needs. — 4 servings · PT15M
